page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8269CY cores running at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA instruction sets. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations position the ecs.sn2.large in the lower performance tiers. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% category. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 164.0 GB/sec (bottom 25%), which drops to a poor 21.24 GB/sec (bottom 10%) for large uncached blocks. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, also rank in the bottom 10% to 25%. Database operations, including Redis SET operations (2.86 million operations per second) and Passmark database tests, consistently register in the bottom 25%.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but modest performance metrics, the ecs.sn2.large offers a predictable resource profile for entry-level workloads where burstable performance is undesirable but high-end compute is not required. The lack of bundled local storage means storage must be attached externally, which is a key architectural consideration. This instance is suitable for light general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale database operations that do not demand high memory bandwidth or intensive parallel processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently ranking in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. Consequently, this instance is appropriate for predictable, low-intensity workloads such as development environments, light web serving, and small-scale database tasks that do not require high-throughput memory or storage performance.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for predictable, entry-level workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.large is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM, suitable for light workloads.
tagline: A dedicated four-core virtual machine with balanced memory configuration for predictable, entry-level general purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs at 2.5 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
No bundled local storage or GPU acceleration
Suited for development environments and light web serving
